message:
feat(tmux): add capture caching infrastructure (ntm-952.6.3)

Introduce data structures for tmux pane output capture caching to
reduce expensive repeated tmux calls during dashboard refreshes and
other high-frequency operations.

New types added to Client struct:
- captureMu: sync.Mutex for thread-safe cache access
- captureCache: map[captureCacheKey]captureCacheEntry

captureCacheKey structure:
- target: string identifying the tmux pane (e.g., "session:0")
- lines: int specifying the capture line count

captureCacheEntry structure:
- lastActivityUnix: int64 timestamp of last pane activity
- capturedAt: time.Time when capture was performed
- output: string containing the cached pane content

Design rationale:
- Pane captures are expensive (subprocess + IPC + string handling)
- Dashboard refreshes can trigger many captures per second
- Caching by (target, lines) key with activity-based invalidation
- lastActivityUnix enables smart cache invalidation via tmux's
  pane_activity format variable - only re-capture if pane changed
- Thread-safe via mutex for concurrent access patterns

This commit adds only the infrastructure; actual caching logic in
capture methods will be implemented in follow-up work.

Part of Performance & Responsiveness sub-epic (ntm-952.6) to ensure
large sessions remain responsive during dashboard/palette operations.
